input.title: strlen in Loop Condition

output.technical: `strlen` is an O(N) operation. Calling it inside the loop condition causes it to be re-evaluated every iteration, turning an O(N) loop into an O(N^2) operation.
output.beginner: You are recalculating the length of the string on every single step of the loop. This wastes a lot of time.
output.analogy: It's like recounting every single dollar in your wallet before paying for each item at the grocery store.
output.solution: Store the length in a variable before the loop, or use `std::string`.
output.prevention: Cache expensive function calls outside loops if the result doesn't change.

output.analogy: It's like recounting every single dollar in your wallet before paying for each item at the grocery store.
output.solution: Store the length in a variable before the loop, or use `std::string`.
output.prevention: Cache expensive function calls outside loops if the result doesn't change.
